description: A type nullability confusion vulnerability exists in V8's WebAssembly exception handling implementation. The root cause is in commit 7cb6188, which changed the type pushed onto the value stack for catch_ref/catch_all_ref operations from the nullable kWasmExnRef (ref null exn) to the non-nullable ValueType::Ref(HeapType::kExn) (ref exn) in function-body-decoder-impl.h. While this change aimed to align with a spec proposal, it is unsound because JavaScript can throw any value including null, which can be caught by these operations. This creates a type nullability confusion: the type system and Turboshaft optimizer treat the caught exception reference as non-nullable, but the actual runtime value can be null. The Turboshaft optimizer then performs optimizations based on the non-nullable assumption (e.g., eliding null checks), which can be exploited when a null JS exception is caught, leading to memory safety violations and arbitrary code execution. The bug requires the --experimental-wasm-exnref flag to be triggered.

fixes.description: Upstream fix patch exported from commit 9997fc01395257cffd7231f14bb4d9fa7eaa9665 for fixed-image builds.
